problem_statement: As GenAI tools generate an estimated 20-30% of enterprise code, organizations lack transparency about which code is AI-generated, creating challenges for quality assurance, security, IP management, and regulatory compliance oversight.
magnitude_evidence: GenAI tools now generate 'an estimated 20-30% of code in many enterprise codebases'; 'Almost all developers (95%) report the use of generative AI in their workflow [24]'
insufficiency_evidence: 'without proper transparency measures, development teams spend an average of 30% more time on debugging and maintenance of AI-generated code components'; deep learning detection models 'achieve accuracy rates of 85-95%... though performance varies based on code complexity and the specific AI models involved'
solution_mechanisms: pure vs. blended AI code classification
Software Composition Analysis adapted for AI code
